creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 229358
preliminary patch for sysutils/screen port

(In reply to Alexey Dokuchaev from comment #5)
> press END key: 347 (expected 360)
So 347 is 0533, which is "kH" (KEY_LL), while it expects 0550, which is
"@7" (KEY_END) per contrib/ncurses/include/Caps file.  Somewhat oddly, KEY_HOME is "kh" (0406) in that file, and "@1" is actually KEY_BEG.

Back in 2008, GNU/Linux users also reported problems with <End> key in various applications: https://savannah.gnu.org/bugs/?23601.  It is claimed that it was finally fixed upstream in https://git.savannah.gnu.org/cgit/screen.git/commit/?&id=17b43961982.

I've modified that change so it does not *copy* the KMAPDEF()s from "kH" to "@7" line, but *moves* them instead.  This fixes the problem for me on FreeBSD.
